Codex final message:
Implemented issue #1873 with a first-class notification recipient model and updated the settings flow around it.

The main changes are in [hushline/settings/notifications.py]([redacted-path] [hushline/settings/forms.py]([redacted-path] [hushline/model/user.py]([redacted-path] and the new migration [d1f0e9c2b7aa_add_notification_recipients.py]([redacted-path] I added `NotificationRecipient` persistence, backfilled legacy `User.email`/`User.pgp_key` rows into one enabled recipient, changed notification delivery to fan out across enabled recipients, split SMTP settings from recipient addresses, and updated the notifications UI to add, edit, disable, and remove recipients. When the last enabled recipient is removed or disabled, notification/content toggles are normalized off. The encryption page now only manages the account/public key and no longer wipes notification routing state.

I also updated onboarding/setup-completeness checks to look at enabled recipients instead of a single email, made account deletion clear recipients, and added/updated coverage in [tests/test_settings.py]([redacted-path] [tests/test_routes_common.py]([redacted-path] [tests/test_settings_common.py]([redacted-path] [tests/test_delete_account.py]([redacted-path] [tests/test_coverage_push.py]([redacted-path] and [tests/test_helpers.py]([redacted-path]

I didn’t run `make lint` or `make test`; this change touches schema/model files, so I left validation for the runner.

Codex final message:
Patched the two reported type-check failures without changing the branch’s recipient behavior.

In [notification_recipient.py]([redacted-path] I switched the new model to the repo’s existing `Model` alias pattern so mypy can resolve the base class. In [common.py]([redacted-path] I narrowed `recipient.email` to a non-optional local before calling `send_email`, skipping any unexpected `None` value.

I did not run `make lint` or `make test` after this change because the fix touched `hushline/model/`, and this branch’s runner is responsible for the schema/runtime refresh plus validation.

Codex final message:
Added an explicit typed constructor to [notification_recipient.py]([redacted-path] so mypy accepts the existing `NotificationRecipient(user=..., enabled=..., position=...)` call sites already on the branch. The constructor follows the repo’s existing model pattern: it passes mapped scalar fields through `super().__init__(...)` and assigns `user` separately.

I did not run `make lint` or `make test` after this change because the fix is in `hushline/model/`; the runner should refresh runtime state and rerun validation.

Codex final message:
Patched the branch in two places.

In [user.py]([redacted-path] `ensure_primary_notification_recipient()` now creates the shadow recipient by appending it on the parent relationship instead of only assigning `user=` on the child. That should unblock the failures where legacy `user.email` / `user.pgp_key` writes were not producing a persisted primary recipient, which in turn affected notification toggles, onboarding completeness, recipient editing, and multi-recipient email delivery.

I also added the missing migration fixture [revision_d1f0e9c2b7aa.py]([redacted-path] so the migration upgrade/downgrade tests have coverage for the new `notification_recipients` table and legacy data copy behavior.

I did not run `make lint` or `make test` because this fix touches `hushline/model/` and the migration test fixture; the runner should refresh state and rerun validation.
